query: What are the core ramping and output level sustainance requirements for non-spinning reserves in ERCOT?
final_answer:
I now have comprehensive information to give a complete answer. Let me compile the response.

# ERCOT Non-Spinning Reserve (Non-Spin) — Ramping and Output Sustainment Requirements

In ERCOT, **Non-Spinning Reserve Service (Non-Spin)** is the operating reserve provided by capacity that is **not currently synchronized** to the grid (offline generation, curtailed online generation, qualified Load Resources, etc.) but can be brought to its full award output within a defined time and held there for a defined duration. The two core physical requirements you asked about are:

## 1. Ramping / Startup Requirement — **30 minutes to full output**

ERCOT's Non-Spin is fundamentally a **30‑minute reserve product**. A qualified resource must be capable of converting its awarded MW of headroom (or curtailed capacity) into full energy output within **30 minutes** of deployment.

- The resource is notified/deployed through SCED (Security-Constrained Economic Dispatch) and must be online and ramping to its Non-Spin responsibility within the 30-minute window.
- The "ramp rate" used by ERCOT to compute the Non-Spin **capability** of each Generation Resource (or ESR) is multiplied by **30** in the System Ancillary Service Capacity Monitor, reflecting the 30-minute delivery requirement:

  > *"Non-Spin capacity of each Generation Resource is calculated as lower of Non-Spin Award, remaining up capability, **30 times Non-Spin Ramp Rate**, and Non-Spin qualification."*
  > — ERCOT System Ancillary Service Capacity Monitor, describing Sections 6.5.7.5 of the Nodal Protocols. (Source: https://www.ercot.com/content/cdr/html/as_capacity_monitor.html)

- The 30-minute notification window is also reflected in ERCOT's own demand-response program guides (CPower ERCOT Non-Spin overview) and in ERCOT's deployment trigger framework, which is calibrated against 30-minute-out forecast net load. (Sources: https://cpowerenergy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/ERCOT-Non-Spinning-Reserves_2022-NB.pdf; https://www.ercot.com/services/comm/mkt_notices/detail?id=c29a08e6-1763-47bb-952f-cb1d9e4c4cb3)

This 30-minute ramp/startup requirement is the defining feature of Non-Spin — it is the slower "replacement" reserve behind the 10-minute Responsive Reserve (RRS) and the still-faster ECRS (10-minute) products.

## 2. Output Sustainment Requirement — **4 consecutive hours at full award**

Since NPRR1096 (approved by the PUCT on May 12, 2022, with the Non-Spin sections implemented in the December 2022 R6 release on Dec 6–8, 2022), a Non-Spin resource must be able to **sustain its awarded capacity for four (4) consecutive hours**:

> *"This Nodal Protocol Revision Request (NPRR) requires Resources that provide ERCOT Contingency Reserve Service (ECRS) to limit their responsibility to a quantity of capacity that is capable of being sustained for two consecutive hours and/or Non-Spinning Reserve (Non-Spin) to limit their responsibility to a quantity of capacity that is capable of being sustained for four consecutive hours."*
> — NPRR1096, Background. (Source: https://www.ercot.com/mktrules/issues/NPRR1096)

> *"On May 1, 2022, the Public Utility Commission of Texas ('PUCT') approved ERCOT-proposed Nodal Protocol Revision Request ('NPRR') 1096, which: (1) increased the capacity responsibility duration requirement for Resources providing Non-Spinning Reserve ('Non-Spin') from one hour to four consecutive hours (i.e., to provide Non-Spin, a Resource must now be able to sustain a capacity responsibility for four consecutive hours)…"*
> — Baker Botts, *Energy Storage Issues in the ERCOT Market* (April 2023). (Source: https://www.bakerbotts.com/thought-leadership/publications/2023/april/2022-energy-memo_energy-storage-issues-in-the-ercot-market)

ERCOT's rationale was that Non-Spin needs to "cover risks associated with net load forecast errors…[and] thermal Resource intra-day Forced Outages," so the prior 1-hour sustainment was insufficient. The 4-hour requirement now applies across the capability, award, and telemetry calculations for Non-Spin.

### How the 4-hour sustainment is enforced in the market systems

- **Generation Resources and ESRs:** Non-Spin capability = min(Non-Spin Award, remaining up capability, **30 × Non-Spin Ramp Rate**, Non-Spin qualification, **sustained for the SCED duration requirement of Non-Spin**). (Source: https://www.ercot.com/content/cdr/html/as_capacity_monitor.html)
- **Energy Storage Resources (ESRs):** ESR Non-Spin capability is further constrained to the amount that can be sustained for **the SCED duration requirements of Non-Spin from ESRs**, which equates to the 4-hour obligation. (Source: https://www.ercot.com/content/cdr/html/as_capacity_monitor.html)
- **ESR State-of-Charge (SOC):** ERCOT's BPM on "ERCOT and QSE Operations Practices During the Operating Hour" requires ESRs to maintain SOC sufficient to meet:  
  `SOC Expectation for AS = AS MWh Responsibility − (AS Energy Deployed since start of Operating Hour + PFR Energy Deployment since start of Operating Hour)`. (Source: Baker Botts memo, citing ERCOT BPM; https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2021/10/13/BP_ERCOT_And_QSE_Operations_Practices_During_The_Operating_Hour_Version_5.16.docx)
- **Unannounced testing:** ERCOT has authority (per NPRR1096 / Protocols §8.1.1.2(19)) to perform unannounced capability tests on ESRs providing Non-Spin; the ESR must immediately demonstrate it can reach the target output and "hold at that output level for a minimum duration required to verify ESR's state of charge capability to meet the…Non-Spin…Responsibility." If it fails, future Non-Spin capacity is limited to the demonstrated amount, and the ESR can request up to two retests per month. (Source: Baker Botts memo, citing NPRR 1096-28 and Protocols §8.1.1.2(19))

## Summary Table

| Requirement | Non-Spinning Reserve (Non-Spin) |
|---|---|
| **Activation / start time** | Up to **30 minutes** from dispatch instruction (SCED/manual deployment) |
| **Ramp to full award** | Must reach awarded MW within the 30-minute window (capability = 30 × Non-Spin Ramp Rate, capped by remaining up capability and qualification) |
| **Sustainment at full award** | **4 consecutive hours** (post-NPRR1096, implemented Dec 2022 R6 release) |
| **Eligible resources** | On-line Generation Resources (with energy offer curves or output schedules, including power augmentation), Off-line Generation Resources (excl. Quick Start, except in OFFQS), Quick Start Generation Resources (OFFQS), Load Resources, Energy Storage Resources (with 4-hr SOC sustainment and unannounced-test eligibility) |
| **Pre-NPRR1096 sustainment** | Was 1 hour — increased to 4 hours effective Dec 2022 |
| **Distinction from ECRS** | ECRS = 10-min ramp, 2-hr sustain; Non-Spin = 30-min ramp, 4-hr sustain |

## Key Sources

- ERCOT Nodal Protocols §3.17.3, §8.1.1.2 — Non-Spin qualification and general capacity testing (referenced via NPRR1096 and the May 1, 2024 Protocols PDF: https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/04/25/May%201,%202024%20Nodal%20Protocols.pdf)
- ERCOT Nodal Operating Guide §2.3.2 / §2.3.2.1 — Non-Spinning Reserve Service (June 1, 2025 edition: https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2025/05/21/June-1-2025-Nodal-Operating-Guide-v2.pdf)
- ERCOT System Ancillary Service Capacity Monitor — defines the "30 × Non-Spin Ramp Rate" capability calculation: https://www.ercot.com/content/cdr/html/as_capacity_monitor.html
- NPRR1096 — "Require Sustained Two-Hour Capability for ECRS and Four-Hour Capability for Non-Spin" (approved 5/12/2022, R6 release 12/6–8/2022): https://www.ercot.com/mktrules/issues/NPRR1096
- ERCOT Market Notice M-C093022-01 (9/30/2022) on ESR Non-Spin qualification and NPRR1096 implementation: https://www.ercot.com/services/comm/mkt_notices/detail?id=c29a08e6-1763-47bb-952f-cb1d9e4c4cb3
- ERCOT Nodal Protocol Section 8.1.1.2(19) — General Capacity Testing Requirements (cited in NPRR1096 PUCT Report)
- ERCOT BPM — *ERCOT and QSE Operations Practices During the Operating Hour* (v5.16, Dec 2022), SOC telemetry and SOC-Expectation formula
- Baker Botts, *Energy Storage Issues in the ERCOT Market* (April 2023): https://www.bakerbotts.com/thought-leadership/publications/2023/april/2022-energy-memo_energy-storage-issues-in-the-ercot-market
- ERCOT presentation, *Frequency Response and Ancillary Services in ERCOT*, J. Matevosyan (NERC workshop, 9/17/2019): https://www.nerc.com/globalassets/our-work/workshops/5-3_matevosjana__pfr_ercot_frequency_response_and_ancillary_services.pdf

**Bottom line:** A Non-Spin resource in ERCOT must be able to ramp from its at-rest state to its full Non-Spin award within **30 minutes** and then hold that output level continuously for **4 hours**. (By contrast, ECRS requires a 10-minute ramp and 2-hour sustain, and Responsive Reserve / Fast Frequency Response products respond in seconds-to-minutes with shorter or different sustainment requirements.)

> — NPRR1096, Background. (Source: https://www.ercot.com/mktrules/issues/NPRR1096)

## Summary Table

| Requirement | Non-Spinning Reserve (Non-Spin) |
|---|---|
| **Activation / start time** | Up to **30 minutes** from dispatch instruction (SCED/manual deployment) |
| **Ramp to full award** | Must reach awarded MW within the 30-minute window (capability = 30 × Non-Spin Ramp Rate, capped by remaining up capability and qualification) |

- ERCOT Nodal Protocols §3.17.3, §8.1.1.2 — Non-Spin qualification and general capacity testing (referenced via NPRR1096 and the May 1, 2024 Protocols PDF: https://www.ercot.com/files/docs/2024/04/25/May%201,%202024%20Nodal%20Protocols.pdf)

- ERCOT BPM — *ERCOT and QSE Operations Practices During the Operating Hour* (v5.16, Dec 2022), SOC telemetry and SOC-Expectation formula
- Baker Botts, *Energy Storage Issues in the ERCOT Market* (April 2023): https://www.bakerbotts.com/thought-leadership/publications/2023/april/2022-energy-memo_energy-storage-issues-in-the-ercot-market
